channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shift towards broader welfare policies raises concerns about targeting and fiscal sustainability in Australia
item.description: The article argues that Australia’s social safety net is undergoing a significant shift under the Albanese government, moving away from its traditionally targeted welfare model towards a broader, more...

item.title: Duterte defence rejects ICC claim of state policy to kill, calls it baseless in war on drugs case
item.description: The defence team of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte has strongly rejected the International Criminal Court’s (ICC) findings that a state policy to kill was behind the killings linked to hi...

item.title: Zanzibar Launches Marine Data Programme to Support Sustainable Ocean Investments
item.description: Zanzibar Government imetoa mpango maalumu wa kukusanya na kusimamia data kuhusu matumizi ya rasilimali za baharini, lengo likiwa kuendeleza uwekezaji endelevu katika uchumi wa buluu. Mpango huu, unaor...

item.title: Ex-domestic help allegedly enters Delhi IRS officer’s home and kills daughter after exploiting trust
item.description: A shocking case has emerged from south Delhi where a 19-year-old former domestic help has been accused of raping and killing the 22-year-old daughter of a senior Indian Revenue Service (IRS) officer. ...

item.title: Gauteng parents close Rabie Ridge primary school over unsafe infrastructure and delayed repairs
item.description: Parents of learners at Dulcie September Primary School in Rabie Ridge, Midrand, Gauteng, have taken the drastic step of keeping their children at home and effectively shutting down the school due to u...

item.title: 12 Kidnap Victims Escape During Military Operation Against Boko Haram in Borno
item.description: At least twelve people wey Boko Haram abduct for Ngoshe community in Gwoza Local Government Area of Borno State don manage escape after Nigerian military carry out operation for one of the insurgents’...

item.title: Egerton University Student Shares Uplifting Text from Laundry Lady Before Exams
item.description: Nelly Khasandi, a second-year student at Egerton University, shared a heartfelt message from her 'mama fua' (laundry lady) ahead of her exams. The message, which was sent via text, offered encourageme...

item.title: Tanzania's Commission of Inquiry Marks a Pivotal Moment for Accountability
item.description: The formation of Tanzania's Commission of Inquiry, following the controversial events of October 29, 2025, is seen as a critical moment in the country's governance. President Samia Suluhu Hassan's dec...

item.title: Senate Advances Budget Plan to Fund Immigration Agencies Amid Homeland Security Shutdown
item.description: The U.S. Senate has taken an initial step toward reopening the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), which has been partially shut down since mid-February, by approving a budget framework to fund Imm...

item.title: Heavy rains cause flooding in Mombasa and Kwale, leaving households affected and one boda boda rider missing
item.description: Heavy rainfall and strong winds have led to widespread flooding in Kenya’s coastal region, affecting communities in Mombasa and Kwale counties. According to the Kenya Red Cross Society, at least 103 t...

item.title: Timeline of bank records allegations and AMLC findings involving the Duterte family
item.description: The article traces a long-running political and legal controversy involving former senator Antonio Trillanes IV, the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC), and members of the Duterte family. It begins ...

item.title: Families of Drug War Victims Hope for Justice as Duterte Faces ICC Trial
item.description: The families of children killed in the Philippines' drug war are hopeful for justice as former President Duterte faces trial at the International Criminal Court (ICC). Grace, whose 16-year-old daughte...

item.title: DSS Arraigns El-Rufai Over Alleged Phone Tapping of NSA Ribadu
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) has brought charges against Nasir El-Rufai, former governor of Kaduna State, before a Federal High Court in Abuja. The charges are related to his alleged involve...

item.title: Debt-ridden property dealer in UP poisons pet and ends life by suicide
item.description: A property dealer from Uttar Pradesh, facing significant financial pressure due to mounting debts, tragically ended his life by suicide after poisoning his pet dog. The man, who had been struggling wi...

item.title: Criminals are commercializing child abuse online and rely on human reporting to be caught
item.description: Online predators who distribute child sexual abuse material are increasingly operating like commercial enterprises, running fully commercial sites across the open web that sell access to vast quantiti...

item.title: U.S. House Oversight Committee Divided on Potential Ghislaine Maxwell Pardon
item.description: Members of the U.S. House Oversight Committee have expressed differing views on the possibility of a pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell, the convicted accomplice of financier Jeffrey Epstein. The committee,...

item.title: Tanzania ranks fourth in global malaria deaths according to World Malaria Report 2025
item.description: According to the World Malaria Report 2025, Tanzania is among the four countries with the highest number of malaria deaths worldwide in 2024. Out of about 600,000 malaria deaths globally, Nigeria acco...

item.title: Fernando Mendoza’s Rise from Overlooked Recruit to Projected No. 1 NFL Draft Pick
item.description: Fernando Mendoza has emerged as one of the most unexpected stars in recent football history, rising from an overlooked high school recruit to a projected No. 1 overall pick in the NFL Draft. Born to C...

item.title: Prince Louis turns eight as the Royal Family releases new birthday portrait from Cornwall holiday
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales have marked their son Prince Louis’s eighth birthday by releasing a new official photograph. The image, taken by trusted royal photographer Matt Porteous during a rece...

item.title: Political Leaders Debate the Potential Risks of Surveillance-Based Pricing in Canada
item.description: Surveillance pricing, sometimes called algorithmic or predatory pricing, has become a political talking point in Canada. Ontario NDP leader Marit Stiles highlighted the practice in a recent video, exp...
